summary: Regression testing of the production bug fix where REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL was 'undefined' on Netlify. config.js with getBackendUrl() fallback logic now correctly resolves the backend URL in all pages. Backend: 11/11 pytest passing. Frontend E2E: full delivery flow (form -> PDF upload -> signature -> save -> success) succeeded. Dashboard and History pages load correctly. Verified via network capture that ZERO API requests contain 'undefined' - all 7 captured requests use the correct REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL ('https://ovosanti-delivery.preview.emergentagent.com/api/...').

config_js_verification.fallback_logic: Confirmed in source: if process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is missing -> uses 'http://localhost:8001' for localhost hostnames, else 'https://ovosanti-delivery.emergent.host' (production fallback). Logic is correct and matches PRD requirement.

critical_code_review_comments: config.js fallback is solid: env var -> localhost detection -> production fallback. Suggest also exporting a small banner/console.warn when fallback path is taken so it's observable in production if env var ever disappears again.
Pre-existing (NOT this iteration): bottom-nav vs Emergent badge overlap on mobile 390x844 (see iteration_2.json). Not related to this config.js fix.
Pre-existing (NOT this iteration): server.py still uses @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') - migrate to FastAPI lifespan handlers.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 additional signed delivery via UI (CFG-001 / Test ConfigJS / Receiver ConfigJS). Dashboard total now 24, signed 13, pending 11.
context_for_next_testing_agent: config.js fix VERIFIED. All API_URL imports now resolve correctly in NewDelivery, DeliveryHistory, Dashboard. No 'undefined' anywhere in captured network. Only remaining UI issue is pre-existing nav-vs-badge overlap from iteration_2 (unrelated to this fix).
rca_of_the_issue: Production bug RCA: In Netlify deployment REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env var was not injected at build time, so axios calls became `undefined/api/deliveries` -> 404. Fix introduces a centralized config.js that (1) prefers process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL, (2) falls back to localhost:8001 for local dev, (3) falls back to the production backend host for any other deployment. All three pages now import API_URL from this central file, removing duplicated URL construction and the risk of 'undefined' leaking into any endpoint.
